title: helianthemum nummularium subsp. pyrenaicum
intro: Helianthemum nummularium subsp. pyrenaicum, also known as Pyrenean rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae, characterized by its small, pink or white flowers and its ability to thrive in dry, rocky areas. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Pyrenean rockrose include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
